__fixture__.name: repair_card_remote_known_hosts_pin_strict_mode
__fixture__.scenario: Remote-scope repair card promoted from a target-cell that resolved to admin_policy_strict_required SSH host-key provenance and reported a fresh host-key fingerprint not yet pinned. The card pins the host-key into the local known-hosts entry the strict-mode resolver consults; the change reaches both the local known-hosts store and the remote target identity, so scope is scope_local_and_remote_target. Authority is remote_target_authority because the act of accepting the host-key is bound to a remote-target trust decision; consent requires step-up authentication so the user re-asserts intent. Cites the network attribution that surfaced the strict-mode rejection and the transport posture object.

title_label_text: Pin remote SSH host-key to known_hosts (strict mode)
what_changes_sentence: Adds the remote target's verified host-key fingerprint to the known_hosts entry the strict-mode SSH resolver consults so future sessions resolve without prompting under the org's strict policy.
where_it_applies_sentence: Applies to the local known_hosts store on this device and to the remote target's recorded trust state; no managed provider, policy bundle, or admin console is touched.

evidence_links.evidence_note: Attribution that surfaced the strict-mode rejection of the unpinned host-key fingerprint.
Posture object naming admin_policy_strict_required as the SSH default provenance.
